title: Hanoi 3-Day City Break
metaTitle: Hanoi 3-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 3-day trip to Hanoi, Vietnam: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum Complex and Temple of Literature. Prices from $1478 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 3 days discovering Hanoi, Vietnam — Humid subtropical climate with hot, rainy summers (May-Sep) and cool, damp, often overcast winters (Dec-Feb) with a persistent drizzle called "mua phun." October to December for cool, dry weather with clear skies. This itinerary blends Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum Complex, Temple of Literature, Hoan Kiem Lake with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
2 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum Complex, Temple of Literature, Hoan Kiem Lake
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Hanoi
itinerary.description: Land at Noi Bai International Airport (HAN) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum Complex — Includes the museum and stilt house. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Hanoi: Temple of Literature & Hoa Lo Prison Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Temple of Literature — Vietnam's first national university, 11th century. In the afternoon, continue to Hoa Lo Prison Museum — Former colonial-era prison, later "Hanoi Hilton". Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Vietnam Museum of Ethnology or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to HAN for your departure flight.

travelTips: Download the Grab app before arrival for transport and food
Learn to cross the street confidently in a steady stream — traffic flows around pedestrians
Book Halong Bay cruises through reputable operators, not the cheapest street offer
Carry small VND notes for street food stalls and markets
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Light, breathable clothing plus a rain jacket for humid, unpredictable weather
A scarf or shawl for temple visits requiring covered shoulders
Comfortable closed shoes for uneven Old Quarter sidewalks
Motion sickness remedies for winding road trips to Sapa or Halong Bay
thingsToDo: Ninh Binh cycling and cave tours
thingsToAvoid: Cheap unlicensed Halong Bay cruise operators with poor safety records
Overpaying taxis without a meter — use Grab or Mai Linh/Taxi Group
Drinking tap water — stick to bottled or boiled water
Crossing multi-lane traffic hesitantly, which increases accident risk
topRestaurants: Bun Cha Huong Lien — Bun cha (grilled pork noodles) ($)
Cha Ca La Vong — Turmeric fish (cha ca), Hanoi specialty ($$)
Banh Mi 25 — Vietnamese banh mi sandwiches ($)
Quan An Ngon — Vietnamese street food collective ($$)
Xoi Yen — Sticky rice (xoi) street stall ($)
nightlife: Hoan Kiem Lake night walking street
instagramSpots: Train Street with a passing train
Tran Quoc Pagoda on West Lake
hiddenGems: Duong Lam ancient village day trip
adventureActivities: Ninh Binh cycling and cave tours
luxuryExperiences: Overnight luxury cruise on Halong Bay
Suite stay at Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi
Fine-dining tasting menu at La Badiane
honeymoonExperiences: Private Halong Bay overnight junk cruise
Sunset cyclo ride through the Old Quarter
Couples spa at a colonial-era hotel
Candlelit dinner overlooking Hoan Kiem Lake
budgetExperiences: Street food crawl through the Old Quarter
Free walk around Hoan Kiem Lake
Bia Hoi (fresh local beer) on plastic stools
localEtiquette: Dress modestly when visiting temples and pagodas, covering shoulders and knees.
Use both hands to give or receive items, especially with elders.
Avoid pointing your feet at people, statues, or altars when seated.
Bargaining is expected at markets but should stay good-natured.
Do not touch anyone on the head, considered the most sacred body part.
localTransportGuide: Grab (ride-hailing and food delivery app) is the easiest way to get around and avoids taxi haggling; the Old Quarter is best explored on foot despite chaotic traffic. Motorbike taxis (xe om) are fast for short hops but require confidence crossing Hanoi's traffic flow.
bestTimeToVisit: October to December for cool, dry weather with clear skies.
weather: Humid subtropical climate with hot, rainy summers (May-Sep) and cool, damp, often overcast winters (Dec-Feb) with a persistent drizzle called "mua phun."
visaInformation: Vietnam requires an e-Visa for most nationalities, obtainable online in advance for stays up to 90 days; a handful of countries (including several in Southeast Asia and parts of Western Europe) have visa-exempt arrangements for shorter stays. Apply for the e-Visa at least a few days before travel.
averageFlightTime: ~10.1h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Hanoi?
faqs.answer: Vietnam requires an e-Visa for most nationalities, obtainable online in advance for stays up to 90 days; a handful of countries (including several in Southeast Asia and parts of Western Europe) have visa-exempt arrangements for shorter stays. Apply for the e-Visa at least a few days before travel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Hanoi?
faqs.answer: October to December for cool, dry weather with clear skies. Humid subtropical climate with hot, rainy summers (May-Sep) and cool, damp, often overcast winters (Dec-Feb) with a persistent drizzle called "mua phun."

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Hanoi safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Hanoi is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Hanoi?
faqs.answer: The local currency is VND. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Noah Costa
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Our trip to Hanoi was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to St. Joseph's Cathedral was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Omar Larsen
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Hoa Lo Prison Museum and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Olivia Costa
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Our trip to Hanoi was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Long Bien Bridge was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Fatima Tanaka
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Hoa Lo Prison Museum alone was worth the trip, but combined with Old Quarter this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Daniel Tanaka
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum Complex and Imperial Citadel of Thang Long. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Garcia
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Hoan Kiem Lake alone was worth the trip, but combined with Imperial Citadel of Thang Long this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Mohammed Sharma
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Thang Long Water Puppet Theatre alone was worth the trip, but combined with Imperial Citadel of Thang Long this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Ava Garcia
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Long Bien Bridge alone was worth the trip, but combined with Bat Trang Pottery Village tour this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Nadia Khan
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Hanoi is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Hoa Lo Prison Museum. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Sofia Garcia
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Long Bien Bridge in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Wei Kim
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. St. Joseph's Cathedral in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Yuki Sharma
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Train Street cafe entry alone was worth the trip, but combined with Bat Trang Pottery Village tour this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Chen Kim
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Train Street in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Sarah Brown
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Hanoi is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Ninh Binh (Trang An) boat tour. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Noah Dubois
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Our trip to Hanoi was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Hoa Lo Prison Museum was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Daniel Williams
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum Complex and Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum Complex. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Omar Williams
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Dong Xuan Market and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Sarah Kowalski
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Hanoi is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Train Street cafe entry. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Liam Ibrahim
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Our trip to Hanoi was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to West Lake (Ho Tay) was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
